codeReview.summary: This fix resolves major parts of the four failed features (banned/can_view_site login rejection, /users/account route, /api/users/:username alias, updated throttle thresholds, and user CRUD group/last-admin invariants), but it does not fully close all prior blocking findings. Blocking gaps remain in public-profile behavior and failed-login delay contract adherence.

issues.description: VAL-USER-051 requires `/users/profile` (empty username) to redirect to home, but this page handles missing `username` by setting an error (`User not found`) and rendering an error state. App routing now maps `/users/profile` to this page (`app/apps/client/src/App.tsx:62`), so the required redirect behavior is still not implemented.

The prior blocking VAL-USER-052 gap remains: profile responses still expose only fixed core fields (id/username/group/joined/last login) and do not retrieve/render extra profile fields with date formatting. UI rendering remains limited to those fixed fields (`app/apps/client/src/pages/PublicUserProfilePage.tsx:78-87`).

Failed-login throttling still returns immediate 429 responses with `retryAfterSeconds` and does not apply a server-side wait before responding. Contract checks VAL-USER-071/072/073 require observed ~1s/~2s/~5s response timing delays, not only retry metadata.

Updated throttle tests validate only `retryAfterSeconds` values (1/2/5) and do not assert elapsed response time, so contract-level delay behavior remains unverified and can pass despite missing server-side delay implementation.

Top nav is permission-filtered, but sub-nav is still selected directly from `SUB_NAV[section]` without permission filtering, so restricted section links can still render when users navigate to those sections by URL.
`createRequireRole` still has no explicit super-admin bypass branch; behavior depends entirely on group-1 permission rows remaining fully permissive in data.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Library route guidance is now stale for account/profile aliases introduced by this fix sequence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:56` still states account edit route is `/admin/account` and warns against `/users/*` links, while app routing now includes `/users/account` and `/users/profile` in `app/apps/client/src/App.tsx:62,110`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `fullstack-worker` skill procedure appears stricter than actual worker behavior and handoff scoring for fix tasks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `SKILL.md` requires red-phase tests and manual `agent-browser` verification (`.factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md:29,54`), but this transcript skeleton shows direct `Edit` steps before new tests and no `agent-browser` invocation for session `394a3bda-47f8-4488-80c8-384f05e5c5a9`, while `handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is `true`.

summary: FAIL — the fix partially addresses round-3 blockers (RBAC login gating, key route aliases, and user CRUD invariants) but still leaves blocking contract gaps: empty-username profile redirect, extra profile field handling, and true timed login-throttle delays.
